question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'rg -n "type Engine struct|type RouterGroup struct|type Context struct|type methodTree struct|type node struct" gin.go routergroup.go context.go tree.go'
graph.answer:
The central runtime is `Engine` as an `http.Handler`.

- `New()` and `Default()` build the engine in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L202) and [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L236). `New()` sets up the embedded `RouterGroup`, pooled `Context`, routing flags, and defaults. `Default()` just wraps `New()` and installs `Logger()` and `Recovery()`.
- Route registration flows through the public API in [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L103) and [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L116): `Handle` and shortcuts like `GET` call `group.handle` in [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L86), which computes the absolute path, merges middleware, and calls `Engine.addRoute`.
- The actual route insertion happens in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L364), then descends into the trie in [tree.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go#L135). That trie is what request matching uses later.
- Request serving starts in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L540) when you call `Run()`, which builds an `http.Server` around `engine.Handler()` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L243). `Handler()` returns the engine itself unless `UseH2C` is enabled.
- The request path is `ServeHTTP` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L662), then `handleHTTPRequest` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L690). `ServeHTTP` lazily normalizes route trees first with `updateRouteTrees` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L517).
- `handleHTTPRequest` does method lookup, path normalization, trie lookup, and then runs the handler chain via `Context.Next()` in [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L198). If no route matches, it falls into redirect / 404 / 405 logic.
- `HandleContext` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L680) is the re-entry path. It resets the context with [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L103), re-dispatches, then restores the old chain state.
- Fallback handlers are wired by `NoRoute` and `NoMethod` in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L326) and [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L332), which rebuild the cached 404/405 chains in [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L356) and [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L360).

What to read next, in order:

- [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L65), [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L72), [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L241), [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L250), [routergroup.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go#L254) for grouping, prefix calculation, and middleware merging.
- [tree.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go#L135), [tree.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go#L288), [tree.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go#L418), [tree.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go#L671) for insertion, wildcard handling, lookup, and case-insensitive / trailing-slash behavior.
- [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L103), [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L122), [context.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go#L198) for reset semantics, copying, and middleware execution.
- [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L364), [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L517), [gin.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go#L690) for the route tree update path and request dispatch core.

Tests I’d read first for behavior changes in this area:

- [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L214) `TestCreateEngine` and [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L467) `TestCreateDefaultRouter` for constructor defaults.
- [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L437) `TestAddRoute` and [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L455) `TestAddRouteFails` for route insertion invariants.
- [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L654), [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L671), [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L708) for `HandleContext` re-entry and middleware re-entry prevention.
- [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L1051), [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L1062), [gin_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go#L1143) for 404/405 and escaped-colon tree normalization.
- [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L145), [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L249) for trailing-slash and fixed-path redirects.
- [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L493), [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L530), [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L545), [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L568) for method-not-allowed and not-found behavior.
- [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L680), [routes_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routes_test.go#L699) for raw-path and unescape flags.
- [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L101), [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L138), [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L394), [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L570), [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L694), [tree_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree_test.go#L714) for trie structure, wildcard conflicts, redirects, and case-insensitive matching.
- [context_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context_test.go#L3501) and [context_test.go](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context_test.go#L3554) if you plan to touch parameter propagation or `Context.Next()` semantics.

I wrote the onboarding memories too; from the project root you can run `serena memories check` to sanity-check them.
